question.text: Under the PML Rules, when identifying beneficial owners of a trust, a reporting entity must identify beneficiaries who hold __________ or more interest in the trust.

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For the purpose of identifying beneficial owners of a trust, the PML Rules require identification of: (i) the author of the trust, (ii) the trustee, (iii) beneficiaries with 10 percent or more interest in the trust, and (iv) any other natural person exercising ultimate effective control over the trust through a chain of ownership or control.
**The Trap:** 25 percent is the threshold for identifying beneficial owners of companies (controlling ownership interest), not trusts. 5 percent and 15 percent are not prescribed thresholds under the PML Rules for trust beneficial ownership. The 10 percent threshold for trusts is lower than the 25 percent for companies, reflecting the higher risk associated with trust structures.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Different entity types have different beneficial ownership thresholds: companies use 25 percent controlling ownership interest, while trusts use 10 percent beneficiary interest. This distinction is a frequently tested concept in NISM XXIV.
**Key Takeaway:** Trust beneficial ownership threshold = 10 percent. Company beneficial ownership threshold = 25 percent. Remember: Trusts have a LOWER threshold because they are considered higher-risk vehicles.
